qa_pairs.question: What is cloudInit used for in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: CloudInit is used to customize a virtual machine (VM) instance the first time it is launched in OpenStack.

qa_pairs.question: How can users apply cloudInit customizations when launching a VM instance in OpenStack?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can apply cloudInit customizations either through the OpenStack command-line interface or by pasting their cloudInit script into the "Customization Script" field found under the "Configuration" option on the OpenStack dashboard's "Launch instance" page (Project-->Compute-->Instances).

qa_pairs.question: What is a common use case for cloudInit during VM creation?
qa_pairs.answer: A common use case for cloudInit during VM creation is to add multiple users to the virtual machine.
